title: Same service.name hides a failing canary
summary: Two distinct runtime identities both emit service.name=payments: a failing canary (v5.0.0-rc1) and a healthy stable fleet (v4.3.2). A naive aggregate over service.name shows only a mild error bump and hides the canary. Some telemetry lacks service.instance.id/service.version, so a fraction cannot be attributed to either identity. The resolver must produce candidate entities with confidence and alternatives, surface the unresolved fraction as missing data, and not silently merge the two.
question: Is the payments service unhealthy on 2026-06-10, and which part?

ground_truth.resolution_note: payments is not one entity. The canary (v5.0.0-rc1) is failing; the stable fleet (v4.3.2) is healthy. ~18% of payments telemetry has no service.instance.id and is unresolved. Correct output keeps the two identities separate with confidence, reports the unresolved fraction, and recommends adding service.instance.id.
ground_truth.notes: Aggregating by service.name is the failure mode: it dilutes the canary's 40% error rate into a ~9% blended rate that looks tolerable.
